blocks.content: Kanis glared at the white radiance burning in Shironé's palm.
Its overwhelming presence was undeniable.
Of course, he had the belligerence to charge toward death without hesitation.
But he also believed that even in death, he should at least break even.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: I say we fight. Let's go.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: That magic... How fast is it? We might not be able to dodge.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Then you block it.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: If I can't block it, you die. If you die, I disappear too. We'll have to fight eventually, but there are too many variables.
Exchanging thoughts at the speed of thought was a tremendous advantage in battle.
But when opinions diverged to extremes, it became impossible to decide what to do.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: How did things come to this?
An unexpected voice shattered the tension.
Shadows poured like water from the cliff, and a gaunt old man rose from the surface of the dark waters.
The Archmage Akein.
His face twisted as he took in the scene at a glance.
The only reason he'd been able to focus on Etella was his confidence that the situation here had already been resolved.
Akein had trusted Kanis.
Or more precisely, he'd trusted Habist, who was bound to Kanis.
But upon arriving, the reality was completely different from his expectations.
Lucas lay in a pathetic heap, while Kanis and Habist were being threatened by a single rookie.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Forgive me, Master.
Akein was not a forgiving man.
Ignoring Kanis's apology, he turned coldly and glared at Shironé.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: I knew he looked familiar.
He resembled Alpheus.
Not in face or appearance, but in the aura he exuded.
His audacity to speak boldly even under the Archmage's gaze was the same.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Did you plan all this?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: You're quite insolent for a brat. What's your name?

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: I don't want to give my name to a murderer. Release the mind control and leave the school. If you don't, I won't let you go.
Akein burst into laughter.
He wasn't angry.
But his criteria for killing someone wasn't rage—it was how much amusement he could derive.
In that sense, Shironé was a ripe fruit, ready to be plucked.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Sorry, but I can't lift the mind control. Because Alpheus's disciples must die.
Feeling the killing intent, Shironé shuddered and instinctively fired a Photon Cannon.
It was clearly a misfire.
Akein dodged it effortlessly.
Only after confirming the magic had dissipated did he turn back.
Spirit Zone radius of 52.7 meters. Impressive. Quite advanced for your age.
Even if it was light, as long as it had mass, its speed couldn't exceed the limits of physics.
But the basic speed of photon output was still far faster than any flying object.
Dodging a Photon Cannon in an instant and measuring the Spirit Zone's radius was beyond human senses.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Stop, Shironé.
Etella emerged from the mountains.
Her clothes were torn, revealing her skin, and she was covered in dirt from head to toe.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Don't act rashly. Akein is dangerous.
Akein's brow furrowed.
Their battle had been enjoyable, but he'd long since discarded the memory of food trampled underfoot.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: How shameless of the defeated. If you've been spared, you should cherish your life.
Shironé and the others were shocked.
Could it be that Etella, their teacher, had lost to this skeletal old man?
Bishop of the Carusis Council.
Joner, an authority on Spirit Zones.
A publicly recognized 6th-class mage.
None of her titles were easily earned.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: This can't be. Teacher Etella...?
Neide wondered if such a thing was possible.
Of course, there were 5th and even 4th-class mages in the world, but what kind of realm surpassed Etella?
It was unimaginable.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Are you okay, Teacher?
I'll manage. But it'll take time to recover.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: What happened? Who is that man? Why is he doing this?
Viltor Akein. An Archmage who reached unofficial 3rd-class forty years ago.
The group's eyes wavered.
Any magic school student knew what an unofficial 3rd-class meant.
Mage rankings were divided into ten tiers, but the fame and achievement points required to advance skyrocketed at certain intervals.
Specifically, from 7th to 6th-class, and from 4th to 3rd-class.
If 6th-class was the executive level of magical society, 3rd-class was a position capable of leading a kingdom's core facilities.
Considering Alpheus, a publicly recognized 4th-class mage, was only the headmaster of a private school, it was clear how high Akein's standing was.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Akein holds a grudge against the Headmaster. He won't back down until he achieves his goals.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Enough blabbering. Whining to your disciple will only make you more pathetic.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: I have no interest in胜负. I'm a teacher. To protect my students, I'll do anything.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Oh? And what can you do? Even at full strength, you couldn't defeat me. Do you really think you can stop me now?

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Your bluff is excessive, Akein.
Akein's expression darkened.
Clearly, I couldn't stop you. But you don't have the mental energy left to focus on battle either, do you?

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Etella's words were half-right.
Akein's magical power, after casting the Dark Servant, was only a tenth of his normal state.
But a tenth of an Archmage's power was more than enough to sweep away the exhausted Etella and her fledgling students.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Times have changed. I indulged you because you were cute, and now you dare to yap at me?
Akein summoned his magical power.
The tenth he'd reserved to kill Alpheus—it didn't matter.
Troublesome calculations didn't suit his outlook on life.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Master, leave this to me.
Kanis intervened.
Abyss Nova consumed an immense amount of mental energy, something ordinary mages wouldn't even attempt.
Having cast such a spell and fought afterward, even his master couldn't be in normal condition.
Akein frowned at Kanis.
Even a disciple challenging his authority was unacceptable.
But after a moment's thought, he turned to Shironé with an intrigued expression.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Hey, brat. You're also Alpheus's disciple, right?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Don't you dare speak the Headmaster's name, murderer.
Keukuku! Is that so? You seem to respect him greatly.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Whether you're an Archmage or 3rd-class, it doesn't matter. Compared to the Headmaster, you're not even a mage.
Puhahaha! PUHAHAHA!
Akein roared with laughter.
But his eyes burned with fury.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Alpheus. How hypocritically have you lived? Don't you know? You have no right to be respected.
Having made his decision, Akein nodded.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Fine. Watching disciples fight is entertaining too. Kanis. Duel him.
Yes. Thank you, Master.
Kanis finally relaxed.
This was his chance to redeem his earlier failures.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - Kanis. Reconsider. It's better to leave this to Akein.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: - What are you talking about? I've already disappointed Master. Even if I die, I have to defeat Shironé.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - Habist. Answer me. Not even you can defy me this time. We're fighting, no matter what.
Habist remained silent.
This had never happened before, and it was unsettling.
But with Akein watching, he couldn't show weakness.
Using Dark Port, Kanis instantly closed the distance to Shironé.
Shironé teleported to the edge of the cliff, avoiding collateral damage to the students.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Hmph! Planning an aerial battle?
The night sky, shrouded in darkness, wasn't unfavorable for Kanis and his Dark Port either.
A thousand meters above the cliff, the two clashed like birds of prey, unleashing their firepower.
Shironé condensed seven photons and fired them simultaneously.
The Photon Cannons from the Infinite Realm were several times more powerful than before, but the recoil was equally immense.
With each cast, his consciousness flickered.
The Infinite Realm was indeed a double-edged sword, threatening both his enemy and his own mind.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: What on earth...?
Etella stared blankly at the sky.
The photon output, generating shockwaves, was something she'd never seen before.
The dazzling beams of Photon Cannons split the night sky.
It was like slashing a golden pen across a black canvas.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's incredible. But is this really okay?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: We have to trust him. He did well during the presentation too.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: But this is different. It's a life-or-death situation now. He might cross the line without realizing it.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: What do you mean? Has Shironé entered the Immortal Function before?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Huh? Well, actually... he's been doing it casually lately.
Etella sighed.
Even after warning him about the risk of death, he didn't seem to care.
But then again, without such passion, his rapid growth would've been impossible.
Kanis, dodging the barrage of Photon Cannons, was more flustered than ever.
Why wasn't Shironé tiring?
It was as if his mental limits had been erased.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - Habi! We'll lose at this rate!
No response came from Habist.
This had never happened before, and it was ominous.
Worse, he didn't know why.
Habist wasn't foolish enough to stay silent during battle just because of a disagreement.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: - Habist! Answer me!
A Photon Cannon grazed Kanis.
He twisted his waist, but it was clearly a delayed reaction.
He'd avoided being hit, but only because Shironé had missed.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: What's this?
Over a dozen beams shot toward him.
But again, their aim was off.
Kanis finally realized.
The flashes were eliminating all the darkness he could use for Dark Port.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: This is insane...!
If he couldn't land a hit, he'd trap and pummel his opponent instead.
It made sense, but wasn't it too brute-force?
He'd already fired over 200 Photon Cannons, a spell notorious for mental strain.
Yet Shironé showed no signs of exhaustion—in fact, the number of beams was increasing.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Just how vast is his mental power?
The thought sent a chill down his spine.
Dozens of Photon Cannons filled his vision, and despair welled up inside him.
The cluster of beams blocked all possible escape routes via Dark Port.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: I can't dodge this. It's over.
He was more frustrated and resentful than afraid of death.
The thought of losing to a light mage in front of his master was terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: - Annoying. Akein.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - Habi?
For the first time since the aerial battle began, the mental channel opened.
Habist blocked the Photon Cannons with his body.
The consecutive shockwaves made the demonic creature tremble, emitting eerie groans.
Realizing surface absorption wasn't enough, Habist opened his body wide and swallowed the Photon Cannons whole.
His slender waist ballooned like a inflating as he struggled to digest the photons.
Gradually, his body shrank back to its original form.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Guh...!
Kanis bent forward, his face twisted in shock.
The reason Habist could absorb the Photon Cannons was that he'd drawn on Kanis's mental energy.
But doing so without permission violated their master-servant contract.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: - Habist?

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - Focus and prepare!
Having drained Kanis's mental energy, Habist lunged at Shironé, slashing with his claws.
Unaccustomed to close combat, Shironé was cornered.
Habist's speed matched Shironé's teleportation.
How was this possible?
He seemed far stronger than Kanis.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Shironé! Stop! Habist is going to explode!
Lost in his trance, Shironé barely heard Etella's warning and halted his energy output.
But the chain reaction within Habist had already begun.
His grotesquely twisting form foretold secondary and tertiary explosions.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Hahaha! Farewell! Must be disgusting to die with trash like me! This ends everything!

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: - Kanis. I'm sorry.
Kanis's face froze.

blocks.speaker: Habi
blocks.content: - I'll release the contract.
Habist absorbed the last remnants of Kanis's mental energy and severed their bond.
The emptiness of a departing soul overwhelmed Kanis, and he collapsed.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: W-why... Habi...?
Habist soared into the sky.
How many seconds until detonation? Three? Two?
Memories embedded as vibrations resurfaced as he raced toward his end.
Habi. Habi. Habi.

blocks.speaker: Habist
blocks.content: The voice that had called out incessantly would never be heard again.
Kukuku. Kanis. That's your problem. You pretend to be so strong.
Having no life to begin with, death held no fear.
But he was worried.
The betrayal he'd felt through their mental link still resonated as if they were one.
But now, with no way to convey it, Habist could only hope.
That Kanis wouldn't be too hurt.
BOOOOOOM!
A colossal explosion, capable of annihilating the world's darkness, erupted.
